title: Cleisostoma complicatum
taxonomy: Cleisostoma complicatum is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Cleisostoma, which is part of the family Orchidaceae. This family is the largest and most diverse group of flowering plants, consisting of approximately 30,000 species. Cleisostoma complicatum is a terrestrial orchid, meaning it grows in the ground, and is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The exact classification of Cleisostoma complicatum at the rank of species is likely to be under review and subject to change as botanical knowledge evolves.

intro: Cleisostoma complicatum, also known as the Cleisostoma orchid, is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its complex and delicate white flowers. Traditionally, Cleisostoma complicatum has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cleisostoma complicatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cleisostoma complicatum is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional Chinese medicine formulas to support respiratory health.
